title: trifolium squarrosum subsp. aequidentatum
intro: Trifolium squarrosum subsp. aequidentatum, also known as the small toad rush clover, is a plant species within the Trifolium genus, which belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or dried extract, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
